description: Dark rufous shorebird , body size a little larger than adjacent Baird's but longer legs and long dark drooping bill . Dark rufous breast and face showing signs of moult to basic plumage , as blotchy white feathers beginning to show . White supercilium evident and back feathers dark gray. In flight in comparison to the Bairds that it associated with could be picked out as a dark bird with a white rump and white upper wing stripe . Observed preening and also feeding by probing into water about two or three feet in from waters edge .
photographer: Photographer: John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond
